Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-26-2019 Continued To Jul-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gillian Jones for Nicole Piccinini.
Belinda Vega appeared by telephone for Domenic Piccinini and Alexandria Piccinini (Objectors).

Ms. Jones stated that the Joseph Piccinini matter is still in litigation with a hearing date of November 12th, that she is asking for an extension of final distribution and that she will file a status report. Status on close of estate is continued to July 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Jones to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steve Bovarnick for Nicole Marie Piccinini (Executor).
James Allen for Nicole Marie Piccinini (Executor).
David Partida for Alexandria & Dominic Piccinini (Objectors).
Stacie Nelson for St. Jude and St. Anthony (Beneficiaries).

The Court stated that the objections are based on the litigation in Stanislaus County. Mr. Partida stated that they aren't sure of the funds in the decendent's estate, that they object to the preliminary distribution to the charitable beneficiaries until the motions for summary judgment and cross-motions are decided in three weeks, that trial is set in September, and the mandatory settlement conference is set in August. In addition, Mr. Partida stated that they oppose, referenced the declaration with attachments, stated that they are not sure of the source of the funds in the estate, that they are sure that the funds didn't come from the Father's estate, and that they are asking the Court to withhold distribution until that action is resolved. Brief discussions are held. Mr. Partida stated that they wanted to know where those funds came from and that he's talking about $9,000,000.00. The Court inquired about the total estate. Mr. Allen stated that the cash is $6,000,000.00, that they will be left with $5,477,000.00, and that they are talking about a small distribution. Ms. Nelson argued that the funds in the estate are Ms. Pesco's and the charities and that the sources aren't at issue. Mr. Partida stated that there was no distribution since the Father's passing, that they are asking for a few weeks or months, and that they aren't asking to hold up administration of the estate. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter not reported.
